info.name: Amazon EC2 API
info.description:
Amazon Elastic Compute Cloud (EC2) provides resizable compute capacity in the cloud. This API enables you to launch and manage virtual server instances, configure networking and security, and manage storage volumes with complete control over your computing resources.

Contact Support:
 Name: Amazon Web Services

query.description: (Required) The ID of the AMI to launch
The instance type such as t2.micro, m5.large, c5.xlarge
(Required) The minimum number of instances to launch
(Required) The maximum number of instances to launch
The name of the key pair to use for SSH access
The IDs of the security groups
The IDs of the security groups
The ID of the subnet to launch the instance into

request.description: Launches the specified number of instances using an AMI for which you have permissions. You can specify a number of options, or leave the default options. When you launch an instance, it enters the pending state, then the running state.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

The IDs of the security groups
The ID of the subnet to launch the instance into

request.description: Starts an Amazon EBS-backed instance that you have previously stopped. Instances that use Amazon EBS volumes as their root devices can be quickly stopped and started.

request.description: Shuts down the specified instances. This operation is idempotent; if you terminate an instance more than once, each call succeeds. Terminated instances remain visible for a short period after termination.

item.name: Amazon EC2 Create an AMI from an Instance

query.description: (Required) The ID of the instance to create the image from
(Required) A name for the new image
A description for the new image
If true, Amazon EC2 does not shut down the instance before creating the image

request.description: Creates an Amazon EBS-backed AMI from an Amazon EBS-backed instance that is either running or stopped. The AMI is created with the root volume and any additional EBS volumes attached to the instance.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ID of the instance to create the image from
(Required) A name for the new image
A description for the new image
If true, Amazon EC2 does not shut down the instance before creating the image

item.name: Amazon EC2 Create a Security Group

query.description: (Required) The name of the security group
(Required) A description for the security group
The ID of the VPC for the security group

request.description: Creates a security group. A security group acts as a virtual firewall for your instances to control inbound and outbound traffic.

item.name: Amazon EC2 Add Inbound Security Group Rules

query.description: (Required) The ID of the security group
The IP protocol name (tcp, udp, icmp) or number
The start of port range for TCP and UDP protocols
The end of port range for TCP and UDP protocols
The IPv4 CIDR range

request.description: Adds the specified inbound (ingress) rules to a security group. An inbound rule permits instances to receive traffic from the specified IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR address range, or from the instances associated with the specified security group.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

query.description: (Required) The ID of the security group
The IP protocol name (tcp, udp, icmp) or number

item.name: Amazon EC2 Create a Key Pair

query.description: (Required) A unique name for the key pair
The type of key pair (rsa or ed25519)

request.description: Creates an ED25519 or 2048-bit RSA key pair with the specified name. Amazon EC2 stores the public key and displays the private key for you to save to a file.

The type of key pair (rsa or ed25519)

item.name: Amazon EC2 Allocate an Elastic IP Address

query.description: Set to vpc to allocate the address for use with instances in a VPC

request.description: Allocates an Elastic IP address to your AWS account. After you allocate the Elastic IP address you can associate it with an instance or network interface.

item.name: Amazon EC2 Create a Launch Template

query.description: (Required) A name for the launch template
A description for the first version of the launch template

request.description: Creates a launch template. A launch template contains the parameters to launch an instance. When you launch an instance using RunInstances, you can specify a launch template instead of providing the launch parameters in the request.
